message:
fix(plan): convert extractor panics to ExtractionError result type

The plan extractor previously used assert!/expect! macros in extract()
and build_plan_node(), causing panics on two recoverable conditions:
- A root e-class with no valid acyclic extraction candidate
- A cyclic candidate surviving cost selection

This converts the extract() return type from (PlanDag, ExtractionCertificate)
to Result<(PlanDag, ExtractionCertificate), ExtractionError> and propagates
errors through build_plan_node via Result. The new ExtractionError enum has
two variants: NoExtractableCandidate and CycleSurvivedCostSelection, both
carrying the offending EClassId.

Additional improvements:
- Empty-children guard for Join and Race nodes in compute_cost: returns
  PlanCost::UNKNOWN instead of choosing the node, preventing a zero-cost
  empty join from silently winning extraction
- ExtractionCertificate::verify now calls dag.validate() before hash
  comparison, catching structurally invalid plans early
- All call sites (tests, benchmarks, fixtures) updated to .expect() or
  propagate the Result

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
